Package org.eclipse.collections.api.annotation.category

package org.eclipse.collections.api.annotation.category
This package contains category annotations for classifying methods in the Eclipse Collections API.

These annotations mirror the foldable regions in RichIterable and serve as a machine-readable classification system for its methods. Each category annotation is itself annotated with Category, which carries the category name and icon.

The following categories are defined, with links to all annotated methods (class-use pages):

  • Aggregating 📊 - Methods that reduce or fold elements into a single result
  • Converting 🔌 - Methods that convert a collection to another type or representation
  • Counting 🔢 - Methods that count elements or occurrences
  • Filtering 🚰 - Methods that select, reject, or partition elements based on a predicate
  • Finding 🔎 - Methods that locate specific elements such as first, last, min, or max
  • Grouping 🏘️ - Methods that organize elements into groups by a key function
  • Iterating 🔄 - Methods that traverse elements of a collection
  • Mutating 🧬 - Methods that modify the state or contents of a collection
  • Testing 🧪 - Methods that evaluate boolean conditions against elements
  • Transforming 🦋 - Methods that apply a function to each element, producing a new collection
  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    org.eclipse.collections.api.annotation
    This package contains interfaces for Annotation API.
  • Annotation Interfaces
    Class
    Description
    Aggregating
    Marks a method as belonging to the Aggregating category.
    Category
    Meta-annotation that identifies the category name for a category annotation.
    Converting
    Marks a method as belonging to the Converting category.
    Counting
    Marks a method as belonging to the Counting category.
    Filtering
    Marks a method as belonging to the Filtering category.
    Finding
    Marks a method as belonging to the Finding category.
    Grouping
    Marks a method as belonging to the Grouping category.
    Iterating
    Marks a method as belonging to the Iterating category.
    Mutating
    Marks a method as belonging to the Mutating category.
    Testing
    Marks a method as belonging to the Testing category.
    Transforming
    Marks a method as belonging to the Transforming category.
